data.title: Critical Metals Stock Steadies After Key Business Update
data.description:
Critical Metals Corp (NASDAQ:CRML) shares are slightly up on Friday as the company faces pressure following a recent business update. Here’s what investors need to know.

  • Critical Metals stock is trending lower. Why is CRML stock retreating?

Are Geopolitical Factors Fueling The Surge?

Critical Metals this week held a business update where it disclosed entering four non-binding term sheets for the offtake of its rare earth concentrates, allocating 100% of the planned production from its Tanbreez project.

Additionally, the company announced a joint venture with a Saudi Arabian industrial conglomerate, aimed at creating a processing facility specifically for the U.S. military-industrial sector.

The company also revealed plans to construct a pilot plant in Greenland, with operations expected to begin by May 2026. This news follows a significant rally in the stock, which was up approximately 20% earlier in the week, driven by geopolitical developments and positive internal updates.

Why Greenland's Tanbreez Deal Could Re-Rate CRML Stock

For investors, Critical Metals is now tied to one of Greenland's strategic rare-earth assets just as the island moves to the center of U.S.–EU geopolitics. The new Greenland framework that defused threatened EU tariffs lowers odds of trade barriers on rare-earth exports, making long-dated offtake contracts easier to finance.

CRML's four term sheets effectively pre-sell 100% of planned Tanbreez output, a deposit with high-value heavy rare earths used in defense and EV magnets. That revenue visibility can support project debt, reduce the company's cost of capital and draw in additional strategic partners alongside its Saudi processing JV.

If Greenland maintains stable, West-aligned policy, investors could begin valuing CRML less as a speculative explorer and more as an emerging link in a government-backed minerals supply chain, with each step toward binding offtake and project funding acting as a potential re-rating catalyst.

Is CRML Stock Set For A Breakout?

The stock is currently trading 45.9% above its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) and 74.2% above its 100-day SMA, demonstrating strong short-term momentum. Over the past 12 months, shares have increased 147.07% and are currently positioned closer to their 52-week highs than lows.

The RSI is at 68.80, which is considered neutral territory, indicating that the stock is not yet overbought. Meanwhile, MACD is above its signal line, suggesting bullish momentum.

The combination of neutral RSI and bullish MACD suggests mixed momentum.

  • Key Resistance: $18.00
  • Key Support: $16.50

Benzinga Edge Rankings

Below is the Benzinga Edge scorecard for Critical Metals, highlighting its strengths and weaknesses compared to the broader market:

  • Momentum: Bullish (Score: 98.9) — Stock is outperforming the broader market.

The Verdict: Critical Metals’ Benzinga Edge signal reveals a classic ‘High-Flyer’ setup. While the Momentum (98.9) confirms the strong trend, the lack of Value and Quality scores warns that the stock is priced for perfection—investors should ride the trend but use tight stop-losses.

CRML Stock Pulls Back Friday

CRML Price Action: Critical Metals shares were up 2.17% at $18.86 at the time of publication on Friday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Image: Shutterstock

data.title: Bitcoin ETF Flows Vs. Price Action Divergence: How Traders Can Capitalize
data.description:
Bitcoin (CRYPTO: BTC) spot exchange traded funds were approved with the promise of unlocking institutional capital and reshaping crypto market structure. Since launch, flows into and out of these products have become one of the most closely watched data points in digital asset markets. Yet price action has not always moved in lockstep with ETF demand, leaving traders divided over what these flows actually signal.

The divergence between Bitcoin ETF flows and spot price behavior is not noise. It reflects how institutions deploy capital, manage risk, and express conviction in an asset that still behaves very differently from traditional equities or bonds.

Understanding this dynamic matters for traders who want to position ahead of larger moves rather than react to headlines.

Why Bitcoin ETF Flows Matter More Than Headlines

ETF flows represent one of the cleanest windows into institutional behavior. Unlike on-chain wallet activity, which can be obscured by intermediaries, ETF inflows and outflows are reported daily and reflect deliberate allocation decisions by asset managers, hedge funds, and financial advisors.

When capital moves into a spot Bitcoin ETF, the issuer must acquire Bitcoin to back new shares. That creates direct demand in the underlying market. Outflows, on the other hand, often force selling or at least reduce marginal buying pressure.

However, flows alone do not dictate short term price direction. They reflect intent, not urgency. Institutions often accumulate during periods of consolidation and reduce exposure into strength, which can cause price and flow data to diverge for extended periods.

Why Price Does Not Always Follow Inflows

One of the most common misconceptions among retail traders is that sustained ETF inflows should automatically push Bitcoin higher. In reality, several factors can mute or delay the price impact.

First, Bitcoin trades globally, twenty four hours a day. ETF demand is only one slice of total market activity. Offshore derivatives, perpetual futures, and spot exchanges still account for significant volume. If leverage is being reduced or large holders are distributing coins, ETF inflows may simply absorb supply rather than drive price expansion.

Second, institutions often scale in gradually. A pension fund or registered investment advisor allocating to Bitcoin for the first time is unlikely to deploy capital aggressively in a single session. Flows may be steady but small relative to overall market liquidity, especially during high volume periods.

Third, macro conditions matter. Rising real yields, equity volatility, or shifts in dollar liquidity can cap upside even when crypto specific demand remains strong. In those environments, Bitcoin ETFs can see inflows while price churns sideways.

What Persistent Inflows Actually Signal

Sustained inflows over multiple weeks tend to signal strategic allocation rather than tactical trading. This is particularly important for longer term investors.

Persistent inflows suggest institutions view Bitcoin less as a short term trade and more as a portfolio component. That mindset change reduces downside risk over time because capital is less likely to exit abruptly on modest drawdowns.

It also changes who owns the marginal coin. When Bitcoin moves from speculative traders into asset managers with longer time horizons, supply dynamics tighten. Coins held in custodial ETF structures are typically not rehypothecated or actively traded, which can reduce circulating supply during demand surges.

For traders, this environment favors pullback buying rather than chasing breakouts. Periods of consolidation accompanied by continued ETF inflows often precede stronger directional moves once macro or liquidity conditions improve.

What Outflows Should and Should Not Be Feared

ETF outflows tend to generate more anxiety than inflows generate optimism. This is partly psychological and partly structural.

Short term outflows do not automatically imply institutional abandonment. They can reflect rebalancing, tax considerations, or hedging activity rather than outright bearish positioning. In some cases, outflows coincide with price strength as investors take profits into rallies.

More concerning signals emerge when outflows persist alongside weakening price structure and declining derivatives open interest. That combination suggests conviction is fading and capital is rotating elsewhere.

The key is duration and context. One or two days of outflows are noise. Multiple weeks of consistent redemptions during a broader risk off environment deserve attention.

ETF Flows Versus Derivatives Positioning

One of the most effective ways to interpret ETF data is to pair it with derivatives metrics.

When ETF inflows rise while futures funding rates remain neutral or slightly negative, it often indicates spot driven accumulation rather than leveraged speculation. That is typically a healthier setup for sustainable upside.

Conversely, when inflows coincide with aggressive long positioning and elevated funding rates, upside can become crowded. In those cases, price may stall even as ETF demand remains strong.

Institutions often use futures and options to hedge ETF exposure, which can distort simple price flow relationships. Traders who ignore derivatives positioning risk misreading the signal.

What This Means for Traders Right Now

The most actionable takeaway is that ETF flows are a medium term signal, not a day trading tool.

Strong inflows during price consolidation suggest accumulation zones rather than immediate breakout opportunities. Weak or negative flows during parabolic price action often signal distribution rather than trend continuation.

For swing traders, aligning with the direction of sustained flows while timing entries around technical support and resistance tends to offer better risk reward than reacting to daily flow prints.

For longer term investors, persistent inflows reinforce the thesis that Bitcoin is becoming institutionalized, even if price action remains choppy in the short term.

The Bigger Picture for Bitcoin Market Structure

Bitcoin ETFs have not eliminated volatility. What they have done is change who participates and how capital enters the market.

Institutional conviction expresses itself slowly, methodically, and often invisibly. ETF flows are one of the few data points that make that behavior observable.

Traders who learn to interpret flows in context rather than isolation gain an edge. They stop asking why price is not moving and start asking who is accumulating, who is hedging, and who is exiting.

In a market increasingly shaped by professional capital, those questions matter more than any single headline.

Benzinga Disclaimer: This article is from an unpaid external contributor. It does not represent Benzinga’s reporting and has not been edited for content or accuracy.

data.title: Silver's Rally Is Steep - And That's Exactly The Point
data.description:
Silver didn't just edge higher — it took off. After months of steady gains, prices accelerated sharply, pushing the metal above the $100 mark and into a very steep uptrend. Moves like this tend to grab attention, not because they signal an immediate reversal, but because they show how tight the market has become.

The rally has been fast, and at times almost vertical, with brief pauses appearing only after the strongest bursts higher. That kind of price action usually reflects a market that's working through heavy demand rather than one that's running out of buyers. In silver's case, both hedging flows and physical demand appear to be doing the heavy lifting.

Silver futures (daily): prices have climbed rapidly in recent months, with the trend steepening as momentum picked up. Source: TradingView.

What’s driving silver – and why this move feels different

What makes silver's move stand out is that it isn't being driven by just one story. Yes, safe-haven demand has picked up as investors grapple with geopolitical tension, trade uncertainty and questions around monetary policy. But silver also carries a strong industrial angle, and that matters in a market where supply has been tight for years.

Unlike gold, silver sits at the intersection of financial hedging and real-world use. When demand rises on both fronts at the same time, price moves tend to be sharper — and harder to fade.

Physical demand is doing a lot of the work

Another key element behind the rally is physical demand, particularly from Asia. As gold prices surged, many investors turned to silver as a more accessible alternative, fueling strong retail buying from Shanghai to New York.

That wave of demand has added pressure to a market already running at a supply deficit, amplifying price moves and making pullbacks shorter and less comfortable for anyone betting against the trend.

This doesn’t look like a leveraged blow-off

Importantly, the rally doesn't show the classic signs of a crowded speculative trade. Positioning hasn't reached extreme levels, suggesting prices haven't been pushed higher purely by leverage or short-term momentum chasing.

That doesn't mean silver can't cool off — fast moves rarely travel in a straight line — but it does suggest the market isn't yet flashing the kind of excess that typically marks a definitive top.

Where the risks start to appear

The biggest risk now comes from silver's own success. Higher prices can curb demand at the margin, especially in industrial uses like solar, where substitution and slower installation growth may start to weigh on consumption.

At the same time, volatility cuts both ways. Sharp rallies can just as quickly give way to periods of consolidation as the market digests gains and reassesses what prices should look like at these levels.

The bottom line

Silver's surge isn't just about fear — it's about tension. Tension between supply and demand, between financial hedging and industrial use, and between momentum and sustainability.

As long as that balance remains tight, silver is likely to stay volatile — and firmly on investors' radar..

Benzinga Disclaimer: This article is from an unpaid external contributor. It does not represent Benzinga’s reporting and has not been edited for content or accuracy.

data.title: No Smoke, New Pitch: Philip Morris Seeks Reduced-Risk Label For ZYN Nicotine Pouch
data.description:
Philip Morris International Inc. (NYSE:PM) shares were trading slightly higher Friday after the company urged a key U.S. regulator to back a modified-risk label for its ZYN nicotine pouches.

• Philip Morris Intl shares are advancing steadily. Why is PM stock advancing?

Philip Morris International said its experts presented scientific findings to the FDA's Tobacco Products Scientific Advisory Committee as part of a modified-risk tobacco product bid.

The MRTP tag would let its U.S. business tell adult smokers that switching fully to ZYN lowers disease risks tied to smoking.

FDA Advisory Meeting

Philip Morris said the full-day Jan. 22 meeting was part of the FDA's scientific review of an MRTP application filed by affiliate Swedish Match USA Inc.

The presentation centered on a proposed claim that switching to ZYN reduces risks of six major smoking-linked conditions.

Those conditions include mouth cancer, heart disease, lung cancer, stroke, emphysema and chronic bronchitis.

FDA materials stated, "The evidence suggests the proposed modified risk claim is scientifically accurate."

FDA also noted that youth nicotine pouch use remains relatively low.

The committee also heard that seeing the claim increased awareness that switching from cigarettes to ZYN may reduce disease risk.

Scientific Evidence and Consumer Insight

Philip Morris said data showed consumers understood ZYN is lower risk than cigarettes, but not risk-free.

The advisory panel reviewed evidence showing ZYN contains far lower levels of harmful chemicals than cigarettes.

Panelists also heard that ZYN encourages complete switching from combustible products while limiting unintended use.

The committee also discussed Swedish snus, which received an MRTP designation in 2019 and is linked to low smoking rates in Sweden.

Regulatory Context

In January 2025, the FDA authorized ZYN nicotine pouches for sale through the PMTA pathway.

If the FDA grants MRTP status, Philip Morris could communicate reduced-risk messaging to adult smokers who switch completely.

PM Price Action: Philip Morris shares are trading higher by 1.09% to $171.91 at publication on Friday.

Photo: Jonathan Weiss for Shutterstock

data.title: Lithium Americas Unusual Options Activity For January 23
data.description:
Investors with a lot of money to spend have taken a bearish stance on Lithium Americas (NYSE:LAC).

And retail traders should know.

We noticed this today when the positions showed up on publicly available options history that we track here at Benzinga.

Whether these are institutions or just wealthy individuals, we don't know. But when something this big happens with LAC, it often means somebody knows something is about to happen.

Today, Benzinga's options scanner spotted 12 options trades for Lithium Americas.

This isn't normal.

The overall sentiment of these big-money traders is split between 33% bullish and 66%, bearish.

Out of all of the options we uncovered, there was 1 put, for a total amount of $40,000, and 11, calls, for a total amount of $481,821.

What's The Price Target?

Analyzing the Volume and Open Interest in these contracts, it seems that the big players have been eyeing a price window from $5.0 to $10.0 for Lithium Americas during the past quarter.

Insights into Volume & Open Interest

Looking at the volume and open interest is a powerful move while trading options. This data can help you track the liquidity and interest for Lithium Americas's options for a given strike price. Below, we can observe the evolution of the volume and open interest of calls and puts, respectively, for all of Lithium Americas's whale trades within a strike price range from $5.0 to $10.0 in the last 30 days.

Lithium Americas Option Volume And Open Interest Over Last 30 Days

Biggest Options Spotted:

About Lithium Americas

Lithium Americas is a pure-play lithium producer. Following a deal with the US government, the firm will own 59% of one resource, Thacker Pass, which is located in northwest Nevada, with automaker General Motors owning 36% and the US government owning 5%. Thacker Pass is under construction and expected to begin production in 2028. Thacker Pass is one of the largest known lithium resources in the world. The project would be the first clay-based asset to enter production, and we estimate it will be in bottom half of the global cost curve. Management plans to develop Thacker Pass into a fully integrated lithium production site, with downstream refining capabilities on site, and will sell into the lithium chemical market.

Present Market Standing of Lithium Americas

  • Trading volume stands at 27,147,843, with LAC's price up by 4.32%, positioned at $6.5.
  • RSI indicators show the stock to be may be overbought.
  • Earnings announcement expected in 63 days.

What Analysts Are Saying About Lithium Americas

In the last month, 1 experts released ratings on this stock with an average target price of $7.0.

  • Consistent in their evaluation, an analyst from Scotiabank keeps a Sector Perform rating on Lithium Americas with a target price of $7.

Options are a riskier asset compared to just trading the stock, but they have higher profit potential. Serious options traders manage this risk by educating themselves daily, scaling in and out of trades, following more than one indicator, and following the markets closely.
